chapter_title: The Bug in the Lantern Code
text: Milo loved two things more than anything else: rainy nights and writing code that made ordinary objects do extraordinary things. In the attic above his family's bookstore, he had built a tiny lab filled with blinking circuit boards, mismatched keyboards, and jars of labeled wires. One evening, while a storm tapped softly against the windows, Milo was finishing a program for a string of paper lanterns he had hung across the room. With the right sequence, they could glow in patterns, pulse like heartbeats, or flash messages in colored light. But when he ran his newest update, the lanterns flickered wildly and then spelled out a strange message he had never written: FIND THE MISSING LOOP. Milo stared, then smiled. Somewhere in his code, a mischievous bug was hiding. He traced variables through nested functions, followed logs like footprints through fresh snow, and finally discovered a tiny mistake buried deep in a timing loop. One number was off by just enough to turn his lantern script into a puzzle machine. When he fixed it and pressed run again, the lanterns steadied into a warm golden glow and revealed a second message across the attic ceiling: WELL DONE, BUILDER. Then a locked drawer in his workbench clicked open. Inside was an old notebook from his grandmother, who had once been an inventor herself, filled with sketches for gadgets, notes on logic, and little challenges she had written for whoever was curious enough to continue her work. Milo held the notebook with both hands as thunder rolled far away and the lanterns shone brighter than ever. The bug had not been a mistake after all. It had been an invitation.
